topic.name: Violence Against Women Legislation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at preventing and addressing violence against women through the establishment of definitions, grant conditions, and support for specific communities.

Document 1: Amends the Violence Against Women Act of 1994 (VAWA) to add or expand definitions of several terms for purposes of such Act
Requires the Office on Violence Against Women of the Department of Justice (DOJ) to establish a biennial conferral process with state and tribal coalitions
Document 3: Amends the Violence Against Women Act of 1994 (VAWA) to add or expand definitions of several terms used in such Act
Modifies or expands grant conditions under such Act, including requirements relating to nondisclosure of personally identifying information

topic.name: Tariff Schedule Amendments
topic.description: Legislative changes to the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States concerning specific chemical compounds and their duty suspensions.

Document 1: suspend temporarily the duty on mixtures containing fluopyram and tebuconazole.
Document 2: suspend temporarily the duty on Ethanol, 2,2'-Y Y6,13-dichloro-3,10-bisYY2- (sulfooxy)ethyl?amino?triphenodioxazinediyl?bis(sulfonyl)? bis-, bis(hydrogen sulfate) (ester), potassium sodium salt.
Document 3: suspend temporarily the duty on 1,3-propanediaminium, N-[3- [[[dimethyl[3- [2-methyl-1- oxo-2- propenyl) amino] propyl] ammonio] acetyl]amino] propyl] -2- hydroxy- N,N,N',N',N'- pentamethyl-, trichloride, polymer with 2-propenamide.

topic.name: Labor Representation in Elections
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative amendments aimed at regulating the procedures and requirements for collective bargaining representation elections overseen by the National Labor Relations Board (NLRB).

Document 1: Amends the National Labor Relations Act to require the NLRB to decide...whether such unit shall be the employer unit, craft unit, plant unit, or subdivision unit.
Prohibits an election less than 40 calendar days following the filing of an election petition.
Document 2: Amends the National Labor Relations Act to require the NLRB to require that no investigative hearing take place until at least 14 days after the filing of an election petition.
Requires the NLRB...to direct an election by secret ballot as soon as practicable but not before 35 calendar days after the filing of the election petition.

topic.name: Tariff Schedule Amendments
topic.description: The documents discuss amendments to the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States regarding the temporary suspension of duties on specific chemical products.

Document 1: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States to extend the temporary suspension of duty on acrylic staple fiber.
with a cut fiber length of 89 to 140 mm and a target length of 115 mm.
Document 2: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States to extend the temporary suspension of duty on ethene, tetrafluoro, oxidized, polymerized reduced, methyl esters, reduced.

topic.name: Surveillance and Intelligence Reform
topic.description: Legislation focused on reforming surveillance practices and improving oversight of intelligence activities in the context of national security.

Document 1: Amends the Foreign Intelligence Surveillance Act of 1978 (FISA) to establish a new process for the FBI's applications to a FISA court for business records.
Requires the FBI to include a specific selection term for production of call detail records.
Document 2: Amends the Foreign Intelligence Surveillance Act of 1978 (FISA) to establish a new process for the FBI's applications to a FISA court for business records.
Prohibits the FBI from applying for a tangible thing production order unless a specific selection term is used.

topic.name: Medicare Legislative Changes
topic.description: Legislative amendments to the Medicare program enhancing access to services for patients.

Document 1: Medicare Patient Access to Hospice Act of 2015
recognize attending physician assistants as attending physicians to serve hospice patients.
Document 2: Medicare Clinical Pharmacist Practitioner Services Coverage Act of 2010
provide for coverage of clinical pharmacist practitioner services under Medicare part B.
Document 3: Medicare Clinical Pharmacist Practitioner Services Coverage Act of 2008
provide for coverage of clinical pharmacist practitioner services under Medicare part B.

topic.name: Health Care Legislation
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative acts aimed at improving access to health care services and coverage for specific populations and conditions.

Document 1: Ensuring Access to Primary Care for Women & Children Act
Amends title XIX (Medicaid) of the Social Security Act (SSAct) to require that the primary care services...
Document 2: Medication Therapy Management Empowerment Act of 2011
Amends part D (Voluntary Prescription Drug Benefit Program) of title XVIII (Medicare) of the Social Security Act (SSA)...
Document 3: Breast Cancer Patient Protect Act of 2008
Amends the Employee Retirement Income Security Act of 1974 (ERISA), the Public Health Service Act, and the Internal Revenue Code to require a group health plan...

topic.name: Endocrine Disruptors in Food and Water
topic.description: Legislation addressing the regulation and screening of endocrine disrupting chemicals in food and drinking water.

Document 1: Endocrine Disruptor Screening Enhancement Act of 2011
identification of endocrine disrupting substances that may be in drinking water for testing
Document 2: Ban Poisonous Additives Act of 2016
bans food containers composed, in whole or in part, of bisphenol A (BPA)
substances that are endocrine disrupting chemicals

topic.name: Clean Energy and Emission Reduction
topic.description: The documents focus on legislative measures aimed at promoting clean energy technologies, reducing greenhouse gas emissions, and enhancing energy efficiency in the United States.

Document 1: Requires the Administrator of the Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) to establish a federal greenhouse gas (GHG) registry
Defines 'GHG emissions' to mean emissions of a GHG, including stationary combustion source emissions.
Document 2: Establishes in the Treasury the Clean Energy Investment Fund
Defines 'clean energy technology' to mean a technology related to the production, use, transmission, storage, control, or conservation of energy.
Document 3: Sets forth provisions concerning clean energy, energy efficiency, reducing global warming pollution
Establishing a cap-and-trade system for greenhouse gas (GHG) emissions.

topic.name: U.S. Immigration Reform
topic.description: The documents discuss proposed legislation aimed at reforming U.S. immigration policies, focusing on family reunification and border security measures.

Document 1: Amends the Immigration and Nationality Act (INA) to establish the fiscal year worldwide level of employment-based immigrants at 140,000
Increases immigration visas for unmarried sons and daughters of U.S. citizens
Document 2: This bill amends the Immigration and Nationality Act (INA) to establish the fiscal year worldwide level of family-sponsored immigrants at 480,000
The bill increases immigration visas for brothers and sisters of U.S. citizens
Document 3: States that passage of this Act recognizes that the primary tenets of its success depend on securing U.S. sovereignty
Directs the Secretary of Homeland Security to implement metrics to measure security effectiveness at ports of entry

topic.name: Transportation Authorization Acts
topic.description: These documents discuss various authorizations and appropriations for federal agencies responsible for transportation, including the Coast Guard and the Federal Aviation Administration.

Document 1: Authorizes FY2011 appropriations for the Coast Guard
Authorizes Coast Guard active duty personnel end-of-year strength for FY2011.
Document 2: Authorizes appropriations to the Federal Aviation Administration (FAA) for FY2010 and FY2011 for operations.
Directs the FAA Administrator to establish a program to utilize undergraduate and technical colleges.
Document 3: Authorizes appropriations to the Secretary of Transportation (DOT) out of the Airport and Airway Trust Fund for FY2012-FY2015.
Requires the Secretary, in cases where appropriations are insufficient to meet FAA operations, to reduce nonsafety-related FAA activity expenses.

topic.name: U.S. Foreign Policy and Sanctions
topic.description: The documents discuss U.S. legislative measures and policies regarding sanctions and diplomatic relations with countries perceived as threats, particularly North Korea, Iraq, and Syria.

Document 1: Continues diplomatic, economic, and military sanctions against the government of North Korea as a supporter of international terrorism
Expresses the sense of Congress that the Secretary of State should redesignate North Korea as a state sponsor of terrorism.
Document 2: Repeals P.L. 107-243 (Authorization for Use of Military Force Against Iraq Resolution of 2002)
the United States should establish a quick-reaction U.S. military force with an over-the-horizon presence in the region.
Document 3: Maintains U.S. restrictions against Syria until the President certifies that Syria has ceased support for terrorism

Imposes specified trade, assistance, admissions, and military sanctions, as appropriate, on persons or countries that transfer goods or technology contributing to Syria's biological, chemical, nuclear, or advanced conventional weapons programs.

topic.name: Tax Reform Legislation
topic.description: This cluster discusses various legislative acts aimed at reforming and simplifying the tax code in the United States.

Document 3: Extends through 2013 for an individual taxpayer whose adjusted gross income is less than the applicable threshold amount the tax rate reductions and other tax benefits of the Economic Growth and Tax Relief Reconciliation Act of 2001.
Modifies individual income tax brackets for 2013 to reduce income tax for taxpayers whose adjusted gross income is less than the applicable threshold amount.

topic.name: Criminal Penalties for Non-Citizens
topic.description: Legislation addressing the legal consequences for non-citizens involved in criminal activities in the U.S.

Document 1: prevents any individual convicted of a felony or crime of domestic violence in a foreign court from possessing a firearm in the United States
No Firearms for Foreign Felons Act of 2013
Document 2: increases criminal penalties for certain removed aliens who illegally reenter the United States
Prohibition Against Criminal Alien Reentry Act of 2007
Document 3: provides for the loss of U.S. nationality by being an unprivileged enemy belligerent
Revoking Citizenship for Terrorists Act of 2010

topic.name: HIV/AIDS Prevention in African-American Communities
topic.description: The documents focus on legislative efforts to authorize funding for HIV/AIDS prevention, testing, and outreach specifically targeting the African-American community.

Document 1: Authorizes the Director of the Office of Minority Health of the Department of Health and Human Services (HHS) to make grants to public health agencies and faith-based organizations to conduct HIV/AIDS prevention, testing, and related outreach activities to reduce HIV/AIDS in the African-American community.
Requires the Director of CDC to expand and intensify HIV/AIDS activities in African-American communities.
Document 2: Authorizes the Administrator of the Substance Abuse and Mental Health Services Administration to make grants to public health agencies and faith-based organizations to conduct HIV/AIDS and sexually transmitted disease outreach, prevention, and testing activities targeted to the African-American community.
Authorizes the Director of the National Institutes of Health (NIH) to research strategies to reduce the transmission of HIV/AIDS in the African-American community.
Document 3: Authorizes the Director of the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC) to make grants to faith-based organizations for public health intervention and prevention activities in the African-American community to reduce HIV/AIDS.
Authorizes the Director of the National Center on Minority Health and Health Disparities to make grants...

topic.name: Alternative Fuel Tax Incentives
topic.description: Legislation providing tax credits and incentives for the production and use of alternative fuels and vehicles, particularly focusing on renewable energy and natural gas.

Document 1: extend through 2014 the tax credit for producing electricity from renewable resources
authorize appropriations for advanced biofuels research
Document 2: an excise tax credit through 2019 for alternative fuels and fuel mixtures involving compressed or liquefied natural gas
tax credit through 2019 for investment in natural gas vehicle project bonds
Document 3: allow an excise tax credit through 2027 for alternative fuels and fuel mixtures involving compressed or liquefied natural gas
requires 50% of all new vehicles purchased by the U.S. government to be capable of operating on compressed or liquefied natural gas

topic.name: Mortgage Disclosure Regulations
topic.description: Legislation focused on amending the Truth in Lending Act to enhance consumer disclosure of points and fees in certain credit transactions secured by real property.

Document 1: Amends the Truth in Lending Act with respect to requirements for disclosure to a consumer of points and fees information
Excludes from points and fees any such reasonable charges even though a creditor receives compensation
Document 2: Amends the Truth in Lending Act with respect to requirements for disclosure to a consumer of points and fees information
Excludes from the computation of such points and fees any escrow for future payment of insurance
Document 3: Amends the Truth in Lending Act with respect to requirements for disclosure to a consumer of points and fees information
Modifies the criteria for exclusion from the computation of points and fees of certain reasonable charges

topic.name: Land Trusts for Native Tribes
topic.description: Documents discuss the conveyance of land to Native American tribes and the establishment of trust lands within their reservations.

Document 1: Authorizes the Secretary of the Interior to accept title to any additional number of acres of real property located within the boundaries of the original 1857 reservation of the Confederated Tribes of the Grand Ronde Community of Oregon
all real property taken into trust within these boundaries... shall be part of the Tribe's reservation.
Document 2: Declares that the United States holds approximately 373 acres of BLM administered land in trust for the Te-moak Tribe of Western Shoshone Indians of Nevada
Makes such land part of the Tribe's reservation.
Document 3: Holds in trust the following lands: for the Te-Moak Tribe of Western Shoshone Indians of Nevada (the Elko Band), approximately 373 acres of BLM land
Makes the lands held for the Tribes part of their respective Reservations.

topic.name: Middle School Improvement Initiatives
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative efforts aimed at enhancing educational outcomes for middle school students, particularly those from disadvantaged backgrounds.

Document 1: Directs the Secretary of Education to make grants to states, based on their proportion of poor children aged 5 to 17
Defines 'eligible schools' as those where a high proportion of middle grade students matriculate to secondary schools with graduation rates below 65%.
Document 2: Directs the Secretary of Education to make grants to states, based on their proportion of poor children aged 5 to 17
Favors LEAs, or partnerships that include LEAs, that serve high proportions of poor children and children attending eligible schools.
Document 3: Gives priority to LEAs, or partnerships that include LEAs, that serve high proportions of poor children and children attending eligible schools
Permits states to make subgrants to LEAs and partnerships that did not receive a competitive subgrant to assist them in applying for competitive subgrants.

topic.name: Housing Assistance and Reform
topic.description: Legislation focused on reforms and assistance programs related to housing, mortgage, and public housing inspections.

Document 1: Amends the United States Housing Act of 1937 to revise requirements for public housing agency (PHA) inspections
Requires an initial inspection before any assistance payment is made.
Document 2: Amends the National Housing Act to modify requirements for the maximum principal loan obligation
Revises eligibility criteria for cash down payment for Federal Housing Administration (FHA) mortgage insurance.
Document 3: Amends the Housing Act of 1949 with respect to guaranteed rural housing loans
Requires mortgagees to engage in loss mitigation actions as an alternative to foreclosure.

topic.name: Post Office Designations
topic.description: Legislative measures designating specific United States Postal Service facilities with commemorative names.

1: Designates the facility of the United States Postal Service located at 220 Elm Avenue in Munising, Michigan, as the 'Elizabeth L. Kinnunen Post Office Building.'
2: Designates the facility of the United States Postal Service located at 100 Admiral Callaghan Lane in Vallejo, California, as the 'Philmore Graham Post Office Building.'
3: Designates the facility of the United States Postal Service located at 442 Miller Valley Road in Prescott, Arizona, as the 'Barry M. Goldwater Post Office.'

topic.name: Veterans and Military Family Support
topic.description: Legislation aimed at providing benefits and support for veterans and their families, particularly in relation to health issues and financial assistance.

Document 1: Amends the Family and Medical Leave Act of 1993 to revise its requirements for exigency leave with respect to employees belonging to the family of members of the Armed Forces
Entitles to coverage by the Act an eligible employee who is a family member caring for a veteran undergoing medical treatment
Document 2: addresses medical evidence reviews in the case of a former member of the Armed Forces who was deployed in a contingency operation and subsequently diagnosed as suffering from post-traumatic stress disorder
review medical evidence of the Department of Veterans Affairs or a civilian health care provider presented by the former member
Document 3: Entitles a surviving spouse to a benefit for the month of a veteran's death if, at the time of the veteran's death the veteran was receiving veterans' disability compensation
Includes under the VA's presidential memorial certificate program an individual who dies during active service

topic.name: Child Abortion Notification Laws
topic.description: Legislation regulating parental involvement in minors' abortion decisions across state lines.

Document 1: prohibit transporting a minor child across a state line to obtain an abortion
deems such transporting to be a de facto abridgment of the right of a parent
Document 2: Makes an exception for an abortion necessary to save the life of the minor
imposes a fine and/or prison term of up to one year on a physician who performs or induces an abortion on an out-of-state minor
Document 3: Defines 'abortion' to mean the use or prescription of any instrument, medicine, drug, or any other substance or device
Requires such physician to give 24-hour actual or constructive notice to a parent of the minor seeking an abortion

topic.name: Environmental Policy and Management
topic.description: This cluster discusses legislative measures related to environmental protection, land management, and disaster response in the context of natural resources.

Document 1: Prohibits construction, operation, or maintenance of the oil pipeline
Deems a permit to have been issued if FERC has not acted upon a permit application within 30 days after receipt.
Document 2: A process is established for declaration of a major disaster for wildfire on federal lands
The President shall establish a wildfire suppression operations account for wildfires on federal lands.
Document 3: Designates specified federal lands in the Monongahela National Forest as wilderness
Requires the Secretary to develop a plan to provide for enhanced nonmotorized recreation trail opportunities.

topic.name: Tax Code Amendments
topic.description: This cluster discusses various amendments to the Internal Revenue Code related to tax penalties, deductions, and credits.

Document 1: exemption from tax penalties for premature distributions from retirement plans
charitable purposes
Document 2: increase the limit on the deductibility of losses from the sale or exchange of capital assets
$10,000, adjusted for inflation after 2009
Document 3: tax credit for adoption expenses refundable
Adoption Tax Credit Refundability Act of 2015
